mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal accelerating consumer demand, which lifts economic growth expectations and inflation pressure, causing the 10-year yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for higher growth and inflation risk.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS 1-month change and 20-day forward yield change is below 0.05 in out-of-sample walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals a tightening labor market that intensifies wage pressure and expected Fed tightening, pushing up nominal 10-year yields.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following a UNRATE cross below 6.0%, the median 20-day forward yield return is not statistically greater than zero (one-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Combined positive surprises in CPI inflation and near-zero fed funds rates compress real yields, creating an eventual upward adjustment in nominal yields as investors anticipate a normalization of monetary policy to counter inflation.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is above 0.2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the average forward 20-day yield return is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which in an expansionary regime with already falling unemployment signals building cyclical pressure that drives the 10-year yield upward.
evidence_refs: fact:U.S. industrial production has been rising steadily since mid-2013
regime_hint: expansionary phase (UNRATE below 6.5% and GDPC1 growing)
failure_condition: In expansionary phases, out-of-sample information coefficient (IC) of INDPRO 1-month change on 20-day forward yield is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) as a forward-looking measure of residential investment signal robust demand that boosts GDP expectations and inflation forecasts, leading to higher 10-year yields over a 60-day horizon as the economic momentum materializes.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead economic activity by 2-3 quarters
failure_condition: Over a rolling 5-year window, the Pearson correlation between HOUST 1-month change and 60-day forward yield change is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.0%, the market anticipates the Fed will raise the federal funds rate sooner to contain inflation, which directly lifts short-term yields and spills over to the 10-year yield over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve has an explicit 2% inflation target
failure_condition: In episodes where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.0%, the proportion of positive 20-day forward yield returns is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) provide a synchronized signal of labor income growth and consumer spending, reinforcing expectations of sustained economic strength that pushes the 10-year yield higher.
failure_condition: In periods when both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month, the 20-day forward yield return is positive in less than 60% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE < 6.5%), any increase in the fed funds rate from its near-zero level is interpreted as a signal of imminent tightening, which immediately raises the 10-year yield as investors reprice term premiums.
regime_hint: tight labor market with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 6.5%, the correlation between weekly FEDFUNDS changes and 5-day forward yield changes is not positive (Pearson r ≤ 0) over any 2-year window.
